mission.title: cloud-native-network: Add seccomp
mission.description: Add seccomp. This issue affects 2+ users.

steps.title: Identify cloud-native-network feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cloud-native-network deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=cloud-native-network
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add seccomp

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cloud-native-network resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Hi, thanks for the project, it's very intresting!
I would like to work on seccomp feature if possible.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add seccomp
steps.description:
Fix #25 

So this turns out to be simpler than I thought, thanks to the existance of `libseccomp`. `runc` also uses `libseccomp` which does all the heavy lifting.

One concern I have is the `seccomp-sys` repo. We don't have a lot of choices for a rust binding for libseccomp and this one looks reasonable. However, it is LGPL, so we may want to roll our own bindings. I used this repo just for prototype. If we are NOT OK with LGPL code into our repo, then we should discuss on what is the right thin

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Add seccomp is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Fix #25 

So this turns out to be simpler than I thought, thanks to the existance of `libseccomp`. `runc` also uses `libseccomp` which does all the heavy lifting.

One concern I have is the `seccomp-sys` repo. We don't have a lot of choices for a rust binding for libseccomp and this one looks reasonable. However, it is LGPL, so we may want to roll our own bindings. I used this repo just for protot. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cloud-native-network installed or the issue environment reproducible.
